notes:
tuy nhiên = “however”, “nevertheless”. It shows contrast between two ideas.

It’s a linking word, usually placed at the start of the second clause to introduce a different or surprising result. More formal than nhưng (but), and often used in writing or careful speech.

Strong connection: nhưng (more casual “but”).  
If you want to sound a bit more formal or structured, use tuy nhiên instead of nhưng.
exampleSentences: Anh rất thích đi du lịch, tuy nhiên anh không có nhiều thời gian rảnh.
Cô ấy đã cố gắng hết sức, tuy nhiên kết quả vẫn chưa tốt lắm.
Chúng ta đã chuẩn bị kỹ lưỡng, tuy nhiên vẫn gặp một số khó khăn.

translation.en: I want to go out, however it is raining.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn đi chơi, ___ trời mưa.

example.en: It was raining heavily; however, we still went to school on time.
example.vi: Trời mưa rất to, tuy nhiên chúng tôi vẫn đi học đúng giờ.

definitions.en: A word used to introduce a contrast or opposition between two statements.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chuyển ý, diễn tả sự đối lập giữa hai mệnh đề.

exampleSentencesTranslated: He really likes to travel, however he does not have much free time.
She tried her best, however the result is still not very good.
We prepared carefully, however we still faced some difficulties.
notes_md:
**tuy nhiên** = “however”, “nevertheless”. It shows contrast between two ideas.

It’s a linking word, usually placed at the start of the second clause to introduce a different or surprising result. More formal than **nhưng** (but), and often used in writing or careful speech.


Strong connection: **nhưng** (more casual “but”).  
If you want to sound a bit more formal or structured, use **tuy nhiên** instead of **nhưng**.

content.sentence: Tôi thích ăn trái cây, ___ không thích ăn rau.

translation.en: I like to eat fruit, however I do not like to eat vegetables.
